title: Top 10 Psoriasis Herbal Creams for Skin Health

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anthraquinones are good for psoriasis because they help reduce inflammation and promote skin cell turnover, which can improve symptoms of psoriasis such as redness, itching, and scaly patches on the skin.
Aloe-emodin is good for psoriasis because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness, swelling, and itching, making it a potential natural treatment for this skin condition.
Flavonoids are good for psoriasis because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce redness, itching, and skin irritation associated with the condition.
Aloin is good for psoriasis because it helps to reduce inflammation and slow down the growth of skin cells, which are two main things that cause psoriasis symptoms to get worse.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis cream is often used to treat psoriasis because it has soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The cream helps to calm the skin and reduce redness and swelling, which are common symptoms of psoriasis. Aloe vera contains compounds called glycoproteins and polysaccharides, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing. When applied to the skin, the cream can help to reduce the appearance of scales and plaques associated with psoriasis. The cream may also help to moisturize the skin, which is often dry and cracked in people with psoriasis. Additionally, the antibacterial properties of aloe vera can help to prevent infections that can make psoriasis symptoms worse. By reducing inflammation, promoting healing, and moisturizing the skin, aloe barbadensis cream can provide relief from the symptoms of psoriasis.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with psoriasis, start by drying the roots of the licorice plant (Glycyrrhiza glabra) in a low-temperature oven or by air-drying them. Once dried, grind the roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Then, mix 2 tablespoons of the licorice powder with 1 cup of a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the licorice powder is fully dissolved. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Next, mix 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/2 cup of shea butter with 1/2 cup of the cooled licorice oil mixture in a separate saucepan. Heat this mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax and shea butter have fully melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before applying it to the skin to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis cream is used to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches to form. This cream is made from the petals of the marigold flower, which has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and redness in the skin. The cream also has moisturizing properties, which can help lock in moisture and soothe dry, itchy skin. When applied to psoriasis patches, the cream can help calm the skin and reduce the appearance of redness and scaly patches. The anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce the frequency and severity of psoriasis flare-ups. Some people find that using Calendula officinalis cream helps to reduce the thickness and scaly appearance of psoriasis patches.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that can help with psoriasis, you'll need dried licorice root, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and a few other natural ingredients. First, steep 1 tablespoon of dried licorice root in 2 cups of boiling water for 10-15 minutes to make a licorice root infusion. Strain the liquid and let it cool down. Then, mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a saucepan and heat it over low heat until the beeswax melts. Remove the saucepan from the heat and add the licorice root infusion, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel. Stir well until the mixture cools down and thickens, then pour it into a clean container and store it in the fridge. Apply this cream to the affected skin 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm the psoriasis symptoms. Keep in mind that it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any new herbal remedy, especially if you have sensitive skin or are taking medications.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica cream, also known as nettle cream, is a natural remedy that can help people with psoriasis. This is because it contains anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling in the skin, which are common symptoms of psoriasis. The cream also has antihistamine properties that help to stop the skin from itching and feeling sore. Urtica dioica cream has been shown to improve the skin's natural barrier function, allowing it to heal faster and preventing further irritation. Additionally, it has been found to reduce the production of cytokines, which are proteins that cause inflammation and can make psoriasis symptoms worse. By using Urtica dioica cream, people with psoriasis may find that their symptoms improve, their skin becomes less irritated, and they experience reduced discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream for psoriasis, you'll need dried licorice root, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, shea butter, and a preservative. First, infuse the dried licorice root in a carrier oil by placing it in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This will help to extract the active compounds from the licorice root into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or in the microwave in short intervals, stirring between each interval until smooth. Then, slowly add the infused oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture, stirring constantly to avoid lumps. Finally, add a preservative to extend the shelf life of the cream, and pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container. Let the cream cool and harden before use. Apply the cream topically to affected areas 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal creams for psoriasis

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana cream is a natural remedy that can help with psoriasis symptoms. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, scaly patches to appear on the skin. Arnica montana has anti-inflammatory properties, which means it can reduce swelling and redness in the affected areas. When applied to the skin, the cream can help to soothe and calm the irritation caused by psoriasis. It can also reduce the thickness of the scaly patches, making them less noticeable. Some people also find that Arnica montana cream helps to reduce the pain and discomfort associated with psoriasis. The cream is often used in conjunction with other treatments to help manage symptoms and improve skin health. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Arnica montana cream can provide relief and comfort to people with psoriasis. The ingredients in Arnica montana cream are gentle and non-irritating, making it a good option for people who are looking for a natural remedy to help with psoriasis symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with psoriasis, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and essential oils like lavender or tea tree oil. Begin by mixing 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root with 1 cup of water in a saucepan, and then bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced and the mixture has thickened. Strain the mixture and set it aside to cool. Next, melt 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the mixture has melted, add the cooled Glycyrrhiza glabra mixture and a few drops of your chosen essential oil. Stir well and pour the mixture into small containers. Let the mixture cool and solidify before applying it to the affected skin areas. Apply the cream 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis cream is sometimes used to help with psoriasis because it's thought to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. This can help to reduce redness and swelling of the skin, which are common symptoms of psoriasis. Saponaria officinalis contains a type of compound called saponins, which are known to have soothing and calming effects on the skin. When applied to the skin, the cream may help to reduce the thickness and scaling of psoriasis plaques. Additionally, Saponaria officinalis cream may help to promote the healing of damaged skin and reduce the risk of infection. Some people also believe that it has antipruritic properties, which can help to reduce itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis. More research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for psoriasis, but some people find it helpful as a natural remedy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream that helps with psoriasis using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, you will need to first dry and powder the root. This can be done by cutting the root into small pieces and placing it in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees Fahrenheit for 2-3 hours, or by air-drying it in a well-ventilated area for several days. Once the root is dry, grind it into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of the licorice root powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or shea butter in a small saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture until the licorice root powder is fully dissolved, then remove from heat and add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to thicken the mixture. Stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated, then pour the mixture into a small container and allow it to cool and solidify. Once the cream is solid, apply it to the affected skin area and massage gently. The licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin, making it a popular natural remedy for psoriasis.

remedies.remedy_desc: Taraxacum officinale, also known as dandelion, has been used for many years to help people with psoriasis. The cream made from this plant is good for several reasons. First, it has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling on the skin. This is important because psoriasis can cause painful, itchy patches on the skin. The cream also has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Taraxacum officinale cream has been shown to have antiproliferative effects, which means it can help slow down the growth of skin cells that can contribute to psoriasis. This can help relieve symptoms and improve the appearance of the skin. Some people have also reported that the cream has a soothing effect on the skin, which can help reduce itching and irritation.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with psoriasis, start by preparing the herbal extract. First, dry and grind 1 part of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root into a fine powder. Then, combine 1 part of the powdered root with 2 parts of a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, allowing the active ingredients to infuse into the oil. After the infusion is complete, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax with 2 tablespoons of the herbal oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture has reached a smooth consistency. Remove from heat and let it cool until it thickens into a creamy texture. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to keep it fresh for up to 6 months. Apply the cream directly to the affected skin area 2-3 times a day to help soothe and calm psoriasis symptoms.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for psoriasis, you will need to start by making a glycyrrhizin extract from the roots of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant. Begin by drying and grinding the roots into a fine powder, then mix it with a solvent like ethanol or glycerin to create a liquid extract. After steeping the mixture for a few days or weeks, strain the liquid and discard the solids. Next, combine the extract with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and add other soothing ingredients such as aloe vera gel or shea butter. Stir well and heat the mixture gently to ensure the ingredients combine evenly, then let it cool before transferring it to a container. To make the cream, whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it becomes light and fluffy, then add a thickening agent like beeswax or candelilla wax to give it a firmer texture. Finally, store the cream in the refrigerator to slow down the growth of bacteria and keep it fresh for a longer period.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum cream is often used to help people with psoriasis. Psoriasis is a skin condition that causes red, itchy patches on the skin. These patches can be painful and uncomfortable. The Hypericum perforatum cream works by reducing inflammation in the skin, which helps to soothe and calm the affected areas. It also has anti-itch properties, which can provide relief from the itching and discomfort associated with psoriasis.  This cream contains a compound called hyperforin, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied to the skin, hyperforin helps to reduce the redness and swelling of psoriasis patches. It also helps to promote healing and reduce the risk of infection. Many people find that using Hypericum perforatum cream helps to improve the appearance of their skin and reduce the symptoms of psoriasis.  Some studies suggest that Hypericum perforatum cream may be as effective as other treatments for psoriasis, such as steroid creams. However, it's essential to talk to a doctor before using this cream, as it can interact with other medications and may not be suitable for everyone, especially those with sensitive skin.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a soothing herbal cream for psoriasis using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, start by infusing the dried root in a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried licorice root with 4 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan, then heat it gently over low heat for about 2 hours, or until the mixture reaches 100°F to 150°F. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 2 to 3 hours, allowing the licorice root to release its active compounds into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bowl, discarding the solids. Next, combine the infused oil with a thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl. Melt the mixture, stirring constantly, until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency. Remove it from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean container. Your soothing licorice root cream is now ready to use, applying a thin layer 2 to 3 times a day to help alleviate psoriasis symptoms and promote skin healing.

remedies.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale cream is used to help treat psoriasis, a skin condition that causes red, itchy patches. They reduce inflammation and help to heal the damaged skin cells that cause the psoriasis patches. This helps to relieve the itching and discomfort of the condition. By protecting the skin and reducing inflammation, the cream can also help to prevent further damage and promote healthy skin growth.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a herbal cream that helps with psoriasis using Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice root, start by making a licorice root infusion. This involves boiling 1-2 teaspoons of dried licorice root in 1 cup of water for 10-15 minutes. Let it cool, then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Next, mix the licorice root infusion with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1:3 (1 part infusion to 3 parts oil). You can also add other soothing herbs like aloe vera or chamomile to enhance the cream's benefits. Finally, whip the mixture with a hand mixer or a whisk until it becomes smooth and creamy. Pour the cream into a clean jar and store it in the refrigerator. remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a type of skin treatment that may help with psoriasis. Psoriasis is a condition where the skin gets red, itchy, and scaly. It happens when the immune system gets confused and starts attacking healthy skin cells. Glycyrrhiza glabra cream has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness and swelling of the skin. The cream also has antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage. This can help to calm down the skin and reduce the symptoms of psoriasis. Glycyrrhiza glabra cream may also help to slow down the growth of skin cells, which can help to prevent the buildup of dead skin cells that can cause the scaly patches associated with psoriasis. Some people find that using Glycyrrhiza glabra cream helps to reduce the severity of their psoriasis symptoms and improve their skin health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal cream that helps with psoriasis, you'll need to start by making a glycyrrhizin extract from the roots of the licorice plant, also known as Glycyrrhiza glabra. Begin by drying the roots, then grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder. Next, mix 1 cup of the powder with 2 cups of water in a saucepan, and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce the heat and let it simmer for about 30 minutes, or until the liquid has reduced by half and the mixture has thickened. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, and discard the solids. Add a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to the liquid extract, and mix well to create a paste. Finally, mix the paste with a moisturizing cream base, such as aloe vera or shea butter, to create a smooth and soothing cream that can help to reduce inflammation and itching associated with psoriasis.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be very helpful in managing psoriasis, a condition that causes red, itchy, and scaly patches on the skin. Two herbs that are commonly used in these creams are Aloe barbadensis and Calendula officinalis. Aloe barbadensis, also known as aloe vera, is known for its soothing and moisturizing properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and itching caused by psoriasis. It also has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm down the immune system's response to the condition. Calendula officinalis, also known as marigold, has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce the risk of infection and inflammation in the affected areas. It can also help to promote wound healing and reduce scarring.  Fixing the issue of psoriasis is important because it can cause discomfort, pain, and embarrassment, which can affect a person's quality of life. Psoriasis can also lead to depression and anxiety if left untreated. By using herbal creams containing Aloe barbadensis and Calendula officinalis, people with psoriasis can find relief from symptoms and improve their skin health. This can help to boost their confidence and self-esteem, and improve their overall well-being.
supplementary_best: To remedy psoriasis, the best combination of herbal creams would be a blend of Aloe barbadensis, Calendula officinalis, and Symphytum officinale. Aloe barbadensis is a natural anti-inflammatory and moisturizer that soothes the skin, reducing redness and itching. Calendula officinalis is an antimicrobial agent that fights off infections and promotes wound healing, helping to reduce the severity of psoriasis symptoms. Symphytum officinale, or comfrey, is a powerful anti-inflammatory that accelerates skin healing, reduces scarring, and promotes tissue repair. This combination is effective because Aloe barbadensis addresses immediate symptoms, while Calendula officinalis and Symphytum officinale target the underlying causes of psoriasis by reducing inflammation and promoting skin repair. This synergy between the three herbs creates a powerful and natural remedy for psoriasis.
supplementary_avoid: If you have psoriasis, it's best to avoid creams containing certain herbs that can make the condition worse. One such herb is St. John's Wort (Hypericum is mentioned, but let's use its full name to avoid confusion). It can cause sensitivity to sunlight, which can trigger psoriasis flare-ups. Another herb to avoid is Goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), which contains a chemical that can make the skin more sensitive. This can lead to more inflammation and redness, making psoriasis symptoms worse. Also, avoid creams with Comfrey (Cynara scolymus), as its roots contain chemicals that can cause skin irritation and allergic reactions. These reactions can worsen psoriasis symptoms, so it's best to avoid creams containing Comfrey. Finally, avoid creams with Witch Hazel (Hamamelis virginiana), as its astringent properties can dry out the skin, making it more susceptible to irritation and inflammation.
supplementary_other_remedies: For people with psoriasis, there are several natural remedies that can help soothe and calm the skin. Aloe vera juice is one such remedy - it's known for its cooling and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and itching. Drinking aloe vera juice can also help to hydrate the body from the inside out, which is important for people with psoriasis who often experience dry skin.  Another natural remedy is apple cider vinegar. Apple cider vinegar has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and redness on the skin. It also helps to balance the body's pH levels, which can become imbalanced in people with psoriasis. To use apple cider vinegar for psoriasis, you can add it to your bath water or apply it directly to the affected area using a cotton ball.  Olive oil is also a natural remedy for psoriasis. It's rich in antioxidants and fatty acids, which can help to nourish and moisturize the skin. Olive oil can be applied directly to the affected area, or it can be added to your bath water for a relaxing and soothing soak.  A warm bath with Epsom salt is another natural remedy for psoriasis. Epsom salt is rich in magnesium, which can help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation. Taking a warm bath with Epsom salt can help to calm the skin and reduce itching and redness.  Some people also find that taking omega-3 fatty acid supplements can help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy skin. Omega-3 fatty acids are found in fatty fish such as salmon and sardines, and they can also be taken in supplement form. Taking omega-3 fatty acid supplements can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can help to reduce symptoms of psoriasis.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with psoriasis often experience other health issues due to inflammation and skin barrier disruption. One common condition is psoriatic arthritis, which causes joint pain and stiffness. Turmeric, rich in curcumin, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce joint inflammation and pain. Ginger, another anti-inflammatory herb, can also provide relief.  Psoriasis patients are also more likely to have high blood pressure, which can be managed with herbs like hawthorn and garlic. Hawthorn helps lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels, while garlic reduces inflammation in blood vessels.  Gastrointestinal issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) are common among people with psoriasis. Licorice root and slippery elm can soothe IBS symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting gut healing.  People with psoriasis are also at higher risk for depression and anxiety. St. John's Wort and passionflower can help alleviate symptoms of depression and anxiety by promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation.  In addition, psoriasis patients are more likely to experience cardiovascular disease due to chronic inflammation. Ginkgo biloba and grape seed extract can help reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health by improving blood flow and reducing oxidative stress.  Herbs like aloe vera and calendula can help soothe skin symptoms of psoriasis, such as redness and itching. Aloe vera has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, while calendula has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects.  People with psoriasis may also experience eye problems like dry eye syndrome. Eyebright herb can help reduce inflammation and promote eye health by improving tear production and reducing eye redness.
